metadata.title: EM Consulting · Huntsman + MIT · Admissions Advisory
metadata.description: Wharton Huntsman Dual Degree and MIT admit. Structured admissions consulting for Chinese families applying to U.S. universities.

hero.title: A structured five-milestone plan for U.S. college applications.
hero.subtitle: Huntsman Program (Wharton + College dual degree, ~50 admits/year globally) · MIT and Wharton dual admit the same cycle. An advisor walks every family through the full application season.
hero.ctaSubtext: Free, no contract. A 90-minute session to assess your child's competitiveness and map out a strategy.

items.title: MIT + Wharton, same cycle
items.description: Most consultants sold their last admit a decade ago. We just got ours. The cycle we're advising on is the one we lived through two months back.

items.title: Five milestones, real deliverables
items.description: Every stage produces a written document and an acceptance checkpoint: positioning deck, profile roadmap, finalized essays, submission checklist, decision framework. We work in milestones, and you see what each one produces.

items.title: We turn down more than we take
items.description: We turn down students we can't actually help. We take the cases where our work changes the outcome.

items.title: Native bilingual communication
items.description: We speak Mandarin natively with parents and English natively with students. Parents don't have to wait for someone to translate, and students don't have to switch registers.

items.title: Founding cohort · max 10
items.description: The free 90-minute diagnostic gives a candid assessment of your child's competitiveness. We accept up to 10 families this application cycle, and the assessment is yours to keep whether we work together or not.

process.title: Five milestones with written deliverables at every stage

milestones.title: Strategic positioning
milestones.deliverable: Positioning deck with core narrative direction, initial school list (8–12), and essay topic map.

milestones.title: Profile optimization
milestones.deliverable: Profile roadmap covering activity adjustments, recommendation strategy, and test timeline.

milestones.title: Essay development
milestones.deliverable: Finalized essays with 3–4 feedback rounds each. All content written by the student.

milestones.title: Application review
milestones.deliverable: Submission checklist with full materials review, mock interviews, and a written parent brief.

milestones.title: Post-submission
milestones.deliverable: Decision analysis framework covering waitlist strategy and multi-offer comparison.

trackRecord.title: Founder credentials and prior mentorship
trackRecord.subtitle: EM Consulting was founded in 2026. Below are the founders' own admissions results, plus the schools where students Eric mentored (before starting the firm) were admitted.

founderCreds.name: Mary Liu
founderCreds.detail: Same cycle: MIT (EA, Astrophysics) and Wharton (ED)

trackRecord.mentorshipLabel: Schools where Eric's mentees were admitted (pre-firm)

testimonial.quote: Eric helped me through the entire process — narrowing my school list, sharpening my extracurriculars, and figuring out my personal statement. I was stuck on what story to tell, and he gave me a framework that made it click. He also reviewed all my supplementals and helped me apply to scholarships I wouldn't have found on my own. It made the whole thing a lot less stressful.
testimonial.attribution: Duke '29 · mentored by Eric before EM Consulting

trackRecord.antiObjection: We have no borrowed admissions credentials to list. What we can offer is the application cycle both founders just walked through, and the reasoning behind every judgment we made inside it.

pricing.title: Transparent pricing
pricing.note: Every engagement begins with a free 90-minute diagnostic. No contract until you decide to work with us. After confirming fit, pricing depends on school count (8–12) and service depth.

audit.name: Admissions Diagnostic Report
audit.description: Standalone written report after a 90-minute session: positioning, competitiveness analysis, strategic recommendations. Purchasable on its own, no obligation to continue.

full.name: Full Package
full.description: Five-milestone engagement covering 8–12 schools, with every written deliverable. Parent updates at each milestone plus additional check-ins during intensive phases.

items.a: We just lived the application cycle we're consulting on. Two months ago, we were sitting across from admissions officers ourselves. That proximity is an advantage, not a liability. Former admissions officers can spot adult-ghostwritten essays; our closeness in age makes it easier for students to write in their own voice.

items.q: What is the refund and cancellation policy?
items.a: Full terms are in our service agreement. Summary: Before Milestone 1 (Positioning Deck) delivery: first installment (50%) refunded, minus a $500 administrative fee. After Milestone 1, before Milestone 3 (Essay phase): first installment non-refundable (core deliverables received); second installment not yet collected. After Milestone 3 commences: both installments non-refundable (most labor-intensive phase). Either party may terminate with 30 days written notice; pro-rata refund applies to milestones not yet commenced. The free diagnostic carries no fee and requires no contract.

items.q: How do you prevent ghostwriting concerns on essays?
items.a: We never write or substantially rewrite any application material. Our work is to discuss topics, suggest outlines, and give feedback on the student's own drafts. Each essay runs 3–4 rounds, and every revision is tracked in writing. If a family wants ghostwriting, we'll end the engagement.

Admissions decisions belong to the schools, not to us. Our fees compensate for strategy, time, and written deliverables, not for outcome. Agencies that promise admission usually lock in schools your child would already have a strong shot at, and package that certainty as a guarantee. We focus on the schools where strategy changes the outcome.

items.q: What if you decline us after the diagnostic?
items.a: The 90-minute diagnostic is free regardless. If we determine fit isn't right, you still leave with a candid assessment of your child's competitiveness. If you want that assessment in writing, the standalone Admissions Audit (see Pricing below) covers the same ground as a formal written report.

items.q: How many hours per week does the student commit?
items.a: It varies by milestone. Month 1 (Positioning): 2–3 hours/week. Months 2–4 (Profile): 1–2 hours/week plus activity time. Months 4–7 (Essay peak): 5–8 hours/week across multiple drafts. Months 7–8 (Review): 3–5 hours/week. Months 8–10 (Post-submission): 1–2 hours/week. Most of that time is the student's own writing and revision.

items.q: How are you different from legacy agencies like New Oriental or Crimson?
items.a: We don't introduce ourselves by ranking competitors. Here's what we actually do: two founders who admitted to MIT and Wharton the same cycle, native Mandarin with parents, five milestones with written deliverables at each stage. Parents can judge fit from there.

contact.title: Start here
contact.ctaDescription: At this point, you've seen the track record, the process, and the pricing. A 90-minute diagnostic tells you where your child's competitiveness sits today. Whether we work together afterward is your family's call.
